explanation: The requirement states that the system must provide a log to include results of the pre-use check. In an LTL formula, 'provide a log' can be mapped to the occurrence of the atomic proposition 'logPreUseCheck'. The phrase does not impose any temporal requirement other than that at some point (eventually) the log of the pre-use check ('logPreUseCheck') should occur. This is captured by the 'F' (finally/eventually) operator. The resulting LTL specification is thus F(logPreUseCheck), meaning: 'eventually, the results of the pre-use check are logged.'

explanation: The statement says that the system shall provide a log to include results of the pre-use check. We want to express that whenever the pre-use check is done (represented as 'preUseCheckDone'), the result should be logged (logPreUseCheck). In LTL, this can be interpreted as: Whenever preUseCheckDone becomes true, eventually (F) logPreUseCheck becomes true. Applying this globally (G), the LTL formula is G(preUseCheckDone -> F logPreUseCheck).
